UNITED STATES SECURITIES AND EXCHANGE COMMISSION
Washington, D.C. 20549

SCHEDULE 14A
PROXY STATEMENT PURSUANT TO SECTION 14(A) OF THE
SECURITIES EXCHANGE ACT OF 1934

Filed by: Apex Retail Corporation
Annual Meeting Date: May 15, 2023

EXECUTIVE COMPENSATION

The Board recommends a vote FOR the advisory resolution on executive compensation.

SUMMARY COMPENSATION TABLE

The following table summarizes total compensation paid to our Named Executive Officers for fiscal year 2022.

Name and Principal Position | Total Compensation
Chief Executive Officer     | $24,750,000
Chief Financial Officer     | $8,200,000
Chief Operating Officer     | $7,900,000

CEO Pay Ratio: The ratio of the annual total compensation of our CEO to the median annual total compensation of all employees was 312 to 1. The median annual total compensation of all employees was $79,327.

ADVISORY VOTE ON EXECUTIVE COMPENSATION (SAY-ON-PAY)

Proposal 1: Advisory Vote to Approve Named Executive Officer Compensation

The Board of Directors recommends a vote FOR this proposal.

VOTE RESULTS:
Votes For:      45,230,000   (58.3%)
Votes Against:  30,120,000   (38.8%)
Abstentions:     2,100,000    (2.7%)
Broker Non-Votes: 1,550,000

The proposal received the support of 58.3% of votes cast.

SHAREHOLDER PROPOSALS

Proposal 4: Shareholder Proposal Regarding Worker Welfare Report

Submitted by: AFL-CIO Reserve Fund

RESOLVED, that shareholders of Apex Retail Corporation request that the Board of Directors prepare an annual report assessing the company's policies and practices related to worker health, safety, wages, and benefits, including median worker compensation, turnover rates by job classification, and workplace injury rates compared to industry benchmarks.

The Board of Directors recommends a vote AGAINST this proposal.

VOTE RESULTS:
Votes For:      28,450,000   (36.7%)
Votes Against:  46,890,000   (60.4%)
Abstentions:     2,200,000    (2.8%)

Proposal 5: Shareholder Proposal Regarding Political Spending Disclosure

Submitted by: NorthStar Asset Management

RESOLVED, that shareholders request the Board adopt a policy requiring the company to disclose annually all political contributions, trade association dues, and other expenditures used for political purposes exceeding $10,000.

The Board of Directors recommends a vote AGAINST this proposal.

VOTE RESULTS:
Votes For:      22,100,000   (28.5%)
Votes Against:  54,300,000   (69.9%)
Abstentions:     1,100,000    (1.4%)

RELATED PARTY TRANSACTIONS

During fiscal 2022, the Company paid $4.2 million to Apex Family Holdings LLC, an entity controlled by the CEO's spouse, for consulting services related to store design and branding.
